title: announce: AnnounceProcessor allows hop_count > MAX_ANNOUNCE_HOPS in gradient table
description:
File: python/src/lichen/announce/processor.py:235-244

The processor accepts announces with any hop_count (0-255) and installs them in the gradient table:

```python
entry = GradientEntry(
    destination=destination,
    next_hop=from_neighbor,
    hop_count=announce.hop_count,  # Could be > MAX_ANNOUNCE_HOPS (15)
    ...
)
self.gradient_table.update(entry, now=now_ms)
```

While the signature check prevents spoofing other nodes' announces, a node could legitimately send its own announce with hop_count=255 (e.g., a buggy implementation or testing tool). This would install a route with unrealistic hop count.

The should_relay() check prevents propagation, but the route is still installed.

Recommend adding validation:
```python
if announce.hop_count > MAX_ANNOUNCE_HOPS:
    return AnnounceResult(accepted=False, should_relay=False, 
                          reject_reason=AnnounceRejectReason.HOP_LIMIT_EXCEEDED)
```
close_reason: Implemented hop count validation in AnnounceProcessor.process() and updated imports to satisfy linter. Tests and lint/typecheck pass. Commit 04cc5fe60
